title: Does an abelian group of type [2,G9,5,11] admit a (990,345,120)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The only repaired residue is an auxiliary order-5 quotient-profile computation. Lean would formalize a side lemma, not the shortest remaining path to a sealed publication packet for the selected row.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the micro-paper lane in its audited state. The main row-closing argument broke at the semisimple-divisibility step for the 11/22 quotients, and the only surviving repaired residue is a small order-5 auxiliary slice that is not close enough to a standalone note.
family_affinity: Hall-quotient rigidity for square-n abelian difference-set residues with mixed prime support.
generalization_signal: The quotient-centering setup may still be reusable, but the claimed semisimple-divisibility collapse fails verification for the order-11 path. Only the repaired order-5 auxiliary computation currently survives.
strongest_honest_claim: The order-5 centered norm-25 quotient equation admits exactly two cyclic-orbit profile types, giving quotient profiles {81,66,66,66,66} and {57,72,72,72,72}; the claimed order-11 and order-22 rigidity theorem is not verified.
paper_title_hint: No standalone frontier title is honest yet; at most: 'Order-5 Quotient Profiles for Hypothetical (990,345,120) Difference Sets in Groups of Type [2,G9,5,11]'.
candidate_theorem_slice: For the order-5 quotient reduction, the centered integer equation has exactly 10 solutions in two cyclic translation orbits, yielding the two profiles {81,66,66,66,66} and {57,72,72,72,72}.
next_action: Move this packet aside rather than expand it into a broader theorem program. Preserve the repaired order-5 auxiliary slice, and continue only if a genuinely new proof route for the actual row-closing obstruction appears.
